message:
fix(vdbe): always clear function caches on engine reset to prevent cross-program contamination

The aggregate_function_cache and scalar_function_cache are keyed by
program counter (PC), making them valid only for a single compiled
program. When the cached VDBE engine was reused across different
programs with preserve_runtime_setup=true, the caches retained stale
entries — a SUM function cached at PC=5 from program A would be
returned when program B looked up AVG at the same PC=5.

This caused:
  - SELECT SUM(val) followed by SELECT AVG(val) → AVG returned
    the SUM value instead of dividing by count
  - SELECT typeof(SUM(val)) followed by SELECT typeof(TOTAL(val))
    → TOTAL incorrectly returned NULL instead of 0.0

Fix: always clear both function caches in reset_for_reuse_impl,
regardless of the preserve_runtime_setup flag. The func_registry
Arc is still preserved (for the same-connection fast path), but
the PC-keyed lookup caches are invalidated since a new program
will have different functions at the same PCs.

This was the root cause of the remaining conformance mismatches in
test_conformance_insert_select_dml (AVG returning wrong value →
DELETE removing wrong rows → wrong COUNT).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
